label: Are there any active aquatic animal production facilities within a 10 km radius of this facility?

survey.name: respondent_role
label: What is the respondent’s role on the facility? (Select all that apply)

survey.name: Years_operation
label: How long has the facility been in operation?

survey.name: Type_production
label: What is the type of production in the facility? (Select all that apply)

survey.name: Production_system
label: What the production system does the facility use? (Select all that apply)

survey.name: Based
label: Where is the facility located? (Select all that apply)

survey.name: Operated_outdoor
label: If on land, does the facility have any part of its production conducted outdoors?

survey.name: Which_species_are_cultured_in_
label: Which species are cultured in this facility? (Select all that apply)
Please list any other species cultured in the facility that are not mentioned in previous question

survey.name: Market
label: What is the main market of the aquatic animals?

survey.name: Type_retails
label: Type of retail (s) (Select all the possible options)

survey.name: Environment_and_surrounding_areas
label: 1. Risk factors related to environment and surrounding areas / External
Are there any active aquatic animal production facilities within a 10 km radius of this facility?
Are the facility boundaries clearly marked with signs?
Are the different production areas clearly marked with signs?
Does the facility have a sanitaty system including handwashing and disinfectin footwear at the entrance?

survey.name: Introduction_of_all_life_stages
label: 2.Risk factors related to introduction of all life stages of aquatic animals / External
Does the facility introduce aquatic animals?
How many times have aquatic animals (eggs, larvae, fry, or adults) been received in the past 12 months?
What aquatic animals are introduced into the facility? (Select all that apply)
Are all required official permits, licenses, and registrations obtained prior to acquiring eggs, fry, or adult aquatic animals?
Is health status documentation required prior to purchasing eggs, fry, or adult aquatic animals?
Does the documentation of health status cover the following agents? (Select all that apply)
Is quarantine applied when receiving aquatic animals?
Are the quarantine facilities cleaned and disinfected before the arrival of aquatic animals?
Is the quarantine zone separated from other production areas?
Are specific work clothes designated for the quarantine area, or are general work clothes cleaned and disinfected after use?
Which of the following actions are taken during quarantine to reduce the risk of disease introduction? (Select all that apply)
Are the aquatic animals screened for diseases of concern after arrival?
Is there a traceability system in place with individual or group identification for each batch, where the origin of the animals is documented?

survey.name: source_of_water
label: 3. Risk factors related to source of water and its quality / External
What water sources are used in your facility? (Select all that apply)
Is the water source shared with other facilities?
If yes, is there coordination between the facilities sharing the same water source for disease control?
If yes, what type of treatment is applied? (Select all that apply)
Is the wastewater treated before disposal?
If yes, what type of treatment is applied to the wastewater? (Select all that apply)
Which of the following water parameters are measured?  (Select all that apply)
How often are the water parameters measured?
Cleaning and disinfection of water supply tanks?

survey.name: fomites
label: 4. Risk factors related to fomites (vehicles and equiment for transport live aquatic animals, quarantine facilities, feed and supplies source and waste etc.) / Externall
What type of feed is used in the facility?  (Select all that apply)
How is the feed administrated in the facility?
How is the feed transported to the facility?
Are the following factors verified before receiving veterinary medicinal products? (Select all that apply)
Are the vehicles and containers used for transporting aquatic animals appropriate for the species, size, weight, and number of animals being transported?
Are vehicles (trucks, boats) disinfected before entering the facility?
How often do vehicles (trucks, boats) arrive at the facility?
Is there a designated parking area for vehicles outside the production area?
Does the facility share equipment with other facilities?
Which the following equipment and materials are shared with other facilities? (Select all that apply)
Are shared equipment between facilities cleaned and disinfected?
Is there separate equipment and materials for handling quarantined aquatic animals?
Are the boxes and bags used to transport live animals disinfected before disposal?
Are the boxes and bags used to transport live animals reused before disposal?
Are the containers used to transport live animals disinfected before and after use?

survey.name: biovectors
label: 5. Risk factors related to vectors (animals and wildlife) / External
Does the facility have barriers (natural or constructed) or fencing to prevent the entry of potential vectors, such as animals and wildlife, that could introduce new pathogens?
Are inspections conducted to check for pests and predators?
Are there nets in place to prevent birds from accessing the animals?
Are there pets (cats, dogs, etc.) on the facilities?
Is aquatic animal waste (e.g., dead animals, entrails of slaughtered animals unsuitable for human consumption) kept away from predators and scavengers?
How is organic waste (fecal matter, uneaten feed, unfertilized eggs) managed at the facility? (Select all that apply)
Are pest control measures in place at the facility?

survey.name: personnel_and_visitor
label: 6. Risk factors related to personnel and visitors / External
Do the facility personnel visit other aquaculture facilities?
What procedures are required for personnel before entering the production facility? (Select all that apply)
When is handwashing required for personnel? (Select all that apply)
Is the protective clothing washed and disinfected?
Are visitors allowed to the facility?
What procedures are required for visitors before entering the facility? (Select all that apply)
Do visitors receive information about biosecurity procedure for visitors?
Is it prohibited for visitors to be in close contact with or handle aquatic animals?
Do visitors document the names of other facilities they have visited or declare no prior visits before arriving at the facility?

survey.name: management
label: 7. Risk factors Related with production and its facilities/ Internal
Does the facility culture more than one species in the same production unit (i.e., polyculture)?
Does the facility have a defined upper limit for aquatic animal density per cage, tank, or unit to be stocked?
How often is the defined upper limit of animal density exceeded?
Does the facility have a designated area for cleaning and disinfecting equipment?
Does your facility have a sanitaty system including handwashing and disinfecting footwear at the entrances and exits of each production area?

survey.name: health_monitoring
label: 8. Risk factors related to surveillance and monitoring health and welfare, vaccination/ Internal
How often is the abnormal behavior of aquatic animals monitored?
How often is feeding behavior monitored ?
How often are clinical signs of diseases monitored ?
How often are necropsies performed as part of the disease monitoring?
Are the aquatic animals vaccinated against diseases of concern?
Has there been any sudden, unusual, or elevated mortality in the facility in the last 12 months?
Have any of the following stress factors been observed in the two weeks prior to the onset of mortality? (Select all that apply)
How often is mortality removed from the production units?
How is mortality managed in the facility? (Select all that apply):
When are diagnostic tests conducted to detect diseases of concern? (Select all that apply):
What techniques are used in the diagnostic process?   (Select all that apply)
How often are diagnostic tests conducted on the aquatic animal population?
When is a disease reported to the Competent Authority?  (Select all that apply)
Who performs the sampling in the event of suspected disease presence? (Select all that apply)
Does the facility have a defined process for handling aquatic animals?
Is the equipment used to handle aquatic animals designed, constructed, and operated to minimize physical injuries?
Does the facility have a defined process for loading and unloading aquatic animals that prevents injuries and minimizes stress?

survey.name: VMP
label: 9. Risk factors related to use and storage of feed and veterinary medicinal products/ Internal
How is the feed stored in the facility?  (Select all that apply)
How are veterinary medicinal products stored in the facility? (Select all that apply)
How often are veterinary medicinal products inspected during storage?
Are the following factors inspected in the feed and veterinary medicinal products storage areas? (Select all that apply)
How long is the feed stored in the facility?
Are veterinary medicinal products intended for other species used on aquatic animals?
What veterinary medicinal products are used in the facility? (Select all that apply)
Are the veterinary medicinal products used in the facility prescribed by a veterinarian?
If not, who prescribes the use of antibiotics in the facility? (Select all that apply)
Are veterinary medicinal products used in accordance with the veterinarian's instructions?
Which of the following antibiotics have been used in the facility in the last 12 months? (Select all that apply)
If antibiotics are used in feed, are they mixed on the farm, or is medicated feed supplied?
What are the reasons antibiotic treatments used in the facility? (Select all that apply)
Is a susceptibility test performed before using antibiotics in the facility?
Are the withdrawal times for veterinary medicinal products known and followed?
Is there visible signage to identify cages or ponds under veterinary treatment?
Are adverse reactions to veterinary medicinal products reported to the Competent Authority?

survey.name: equipment
label: 10. Risk factors related to equiment, desinfection and mangement /Internal
Are equipment/materials shared between ponds/cages within the same facility?
Are shared equipment/materials between different tanks/cages in the same facility cleaned and disinfected?
Are there separate equipment for handling dead/moribound aquatic animals?
Are the facilities/nets/cages for holding animals cleaned and disinfected before introducing new animals?
Are the facilities/nets/cages for holding animals cleaned and disinfected after the animals leave the facilities?
Is fallowing conducted before introducing new animals?
Is soil treatment carried out in earth ponds?
How often is the equipment used for handling aquatic animals cleaned and disinfected?
Does the facility use pesticides, insecticides, piscicides, or herbicide treatments?
If yes, how are these treatments applied?
Does the facility use fertilizers, probiotics, or prebiotics treatments?
Does the facility use fresh feces from other animals as fertilizer for the ponds?
Is debris and waste removed regularly from the facility?
Do personnel always wash and sanitize their hands after contact with sick or dead aquatic animals?
Are personnel familiar with or trained in the following topics?
How often do personnel receive training on biosecurity?

survey.name: SOP
label: 11. Risk factors related to standard operating procedures (SOPs) and recordkeeping /Internal
Has the facility implemented a biosecurity plan?
Who developed or verified the biosecurity plan?  (Select all that apply)
Do personnel have access to the biosecurity plan?
How often is the biosecurity plan reviewed?
Do you keep a record of the following routine practices? (Select all that apply)
How long are these records kept?
Do you have written Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for the following routine practices? (Select all that apply)
How are the records available? (Select all that apply)
